title: Some of Mu'awiyah's Affairs and Conduct
description: I was told by Ahmad b. Zuhayr-' Ali: When allegiance was rendered to Mu 'awiyah as Caliph, he put Qays b. Hamzah alHamdani in charge of his police. Then he dismissed him and appointed Zumayl b. 'Amr al -'Udhri,571 or al- Saksaki. Mu'awiyah's secretary and the person in charge of his business was Sarjun b. Mansur al-Rumi. One of his mawali called al-Mukhtar was in charge of his guard (haras). This is also said to have been a man called Malik, who was nicknamed Abu al-Mukhariq, a mawla of Himyar.572 Mu'awiyah was the first to make use of bodyguards.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 18
